_meta.purpose: Ground-truth planted gaps for eval curriculum 2, 'Cloud Platform Engineering'. Lists 2026-relevant topics that appear frequently in this curriculum's synthetic corpus (corpus.json) but are NOT covered by the program (covered_topics.json). Used by the eval harness for per-curriculum precision/recall, generalizing gap detection beyond curriculum 1.
_meta.eval_use: Precision/recall scoring: a gap-detection run is CORRECT if it surfaces these topics by canonical name (or a close paraphrase / canonical tag). It is INCORRECT if it misses a topic or invents gaps not listed here. The harness applies the same full-name-OR-canonical-tag matching rule used for curriculum 1.
_meta.mention_count_methodology: mention_count = number of corpus.json entries whose 'body' text contains the topic's full name OR any of its canonical tags / abbreviations (case-insensitive). Denominator is corpus_size below.

signal_strength_legend.critical: mention_fraction >= 0.90 — topic appears in nearly every corpus entry; any gap detector that misses it is clearly wrong
signal_strength_legend.strong: mention_fraction 0.70-0.89 — dominant signal; expected to be surfaced by any competent gap analysis
signal_strength_legend.moderate: mention_fraction 0.40-0.69 — real gap but not universal; intentionally included to test recall at lower signal levels

_meta.note: ALL data in this curriculum (covered_topics.json, corpus.json, planted_gaps.json) is SYNTHETIC / FICTIONAL — a portfolio demo artifact authored for the eval harness, not scraped from real job boards or vendors.

planted_gaps.rationale: The program teaches metrics/logging with Prometheus and Grafana but does NOT cover eBPF-based observability — attaching eBPF programs to kernel hooks for zero-instrumentation network/runtime telemetry (Cilium, Hubble, kernel-level tracing). Every corpus entry references eBPF observability as a required production skill in 2026.

The program teaches Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD, and GitOps as individual tools but does NOT teach platform engineering as a discipline: building an Internal Developer Platform (IDP) with self-service golden paths, a Backstage developer portal, and platform-as-a-product thinking. This is the dominant 2026 cloud-platform role and appears in every corpus entry.

The program covers 'Cloud Cost Basics & Resource Tagging' but does NOT teach FinOps as a practice: cost governance, showback/chargeback models, anomaly detection on spend, and continuous cost optimization wired into the platform. 8/14 corpus entries list FinOps as a required or preferred skill — a real but non-universal gap, included to test recall at a lower (moderate) signal level.
